stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
Key associated technologies include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to reframe LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of current monotonic scaling laws by incorporating non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining and quantization degradation.
Uses the Shannon-Hartley theorem to provide a unified theoretical framework for understanding model capacity and scaling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the ongoing community interest and benchmarking of Meta's Llama models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its use for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing performance on Apple hardware.
This represents a significant step in making local, high-performance AI accessible on Mac devices.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is enabling private, on-device AI functionality for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed specifically for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in 20B and 120B sizes and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing new methods to exploit the 'personalities' embedded within AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from simpler, earlier forms of AI hacking.
This trend highlights growing security risks associated with advanced conversational AI models.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to target an ICEYE radarsat.
The described capability is noted as unusual for standard satellite missions.
The story touches upon advanced space technology and potential military/surveillance implications.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to theoretically model LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of existing monotonic scaling laws by incorporating non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining and quantization degradation.
Grounds the framework in the Shannon-Hartley theorem, offering a unified perspective on model capacity and scaling.

stories.title: DeepSeek reasonix, DeepSeek native coding agent with high caching and low cost
stories.author: Alifatisk
bullet_points: DeepSeek has released Reasonix, a native coding agent designed for efficiency.
The agent features high caching capabilities and is noted for its low operational cost.
The announcement is relevant to the ongoing competitive landscape of AI coding tools and models.

stories.title: Building an Autonomous SRE Agent: From Raw Telemetry to Safe, AI-Driven Remediation
stories.author: faizanhussainrabbani
bullet_points: Introduces an Autonomous SRE Agent designed to automate the entire incident response lifecycle.
The system leverages AI to move from raw telemetry data through detection, diagnosis, and remediation.
Focuses on creating a closed-loop system that not only fixes issues but also learns from them.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation functionality.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tools and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo is established as the dominant leader in the robotaxi market with a large operational fleet.
The article explores the strategic advantage Nuro might gain by being a 'second mover' in the industry.
Nuro is positioned as a competitor against major players like Tesla, Zoox, and Motional.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was mostly successful, but remains a work in progress.
The vehicle has not yet achieved a full flight to low-Earth orbit.
The report suggests significant further testing and development are required for Starship.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the unauthorized recreation of deceased pilots' voices using technology.
This activity involves circumventing regulations that restrict the disclosure of cockpit audio recordings by the NTSB.
It highlights the intersection of advanced voice synthesis technology and aviation safety/privacy laws.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new paradigm for agent skill development, moving beyond current methods like hand-crafting or loose self-revision.
The core idea is to train agent skills as an external state, applying optimization discipline similar to weight-space optimization in deep learning.
This represents a systematic, controllable text-space optimization approach aimed at reliably improving agent performance under feedback.

stories.title: From Raw Experience to Skill Consumption: A Systematic Study of Model-Generated Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: Language agents are improving by reusing 'skills,' which are structured procedures distilled from past experience.
Domain-level and model-generated skills are highlighted as promising for fast, scalable adaptation within specific domains.
Despite growing extraction methods, comprehensive understanding of these skills remains limited, according to the study.

stories.title: SPACENUM: Revisiting Spatial Numerical Understanding in VLMs
stories.author: Han Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of ensuring numerical outputs (like action magnitudes and coordinates) from Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are genuinely grounded in spatial perception.
The authors introduce SpaceNum, a unified framework designed to revisit and test spatial numerical understanding in VLMs.
The framework captures two complementary settings to analyze how numbers function within embodied environments.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of purely textual Chain-of-Thought reasoning in Multimodal LLMs for complex visual tasks.
Proposes 'ETCHR,' a novel approach that decouples reasoning from image generation by using a dedicated image editing model.
Aims to overcome existing bottlenecks in 'think with images' paradigms, such as fixed toolkits or noisy intermediate images.

stories.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
stories.author: Joydeep Chandra
bullet_points: CHRONOS addresses three coupled failures in static temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces: stale indexing, inaccurate Shapley pricing, and differential-privacy budget over-consumption.
The proposed solution is a three-layer architecture designed to unify the treatment of these temporal data challenges.
Key technical components include using neural-ODE temporal decay for shortcut edges and maintaining explicit public/private data separation.

stories.title: PGT: Procedurally Generated Tasks for improving visual grounding in MLLMs
stories.author: Adriana Romero-Soriano
bullet_points: Proposes Procedurally Generated Tasks (PGT) to enhance fine-grained visual grounding in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
PGT works by overlaying unambiguous geometric primitives onto images to generate dense supervision.
The framework also functions as a low-cost diagnostic tool to pinpoint the source of MLLM perception failures.

stories.title: Human Decision-Making with Persuasive and Narrative LLM Explanations
stories.author: Murat Kantarcioglu
bullet_points: LLMs can enhance human decision-making in classification tasks beyond just accurate predictions.
The research focuses on the impact of generating cogent, narrative explanations from LLMs.
While prior work shows people find AI narratives trustworthy, the effect on objective decision-making is less understood.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling to build reliable AI capable of counterfactual reasoning.
The proposed FM-CGM is a modular framework designed to integrate causal reasoning with pretrained foundation models.
It aims to leverage the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of large models for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.

stories.title: Complete-muE: Optimal Hyperparameter Transfer and Scaling for MoE Models
stories.author: Yan Kang
bullet_points: Introduces Complete-muE, a new framework for hyperparameter transfer in transformer models.
Specifically addresses the challenges of transferring hyperparameters between dense Feed-Forward Networks (FFN) and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) setups.
Overcomes limitations of existing tools by handling architectural changes (Dense to MoE) and scaling variations (MoE total experts).

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: Addresses the quadratic computational cost of Visual Geometry Transformers (VGTs) in 3D reconstruction.
Proposes a strategy to improve efficiency by restricting the number of key/value tokens used in attention mechanisms.
Focuses on making powerful VGT architectures more scalable for multi-view 3D attribute prediction.

stories.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
stories.author: Joydeep Chandra
bullet_points: CHRONOS addresses three coupled failures in temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces: stale indexing, misattributed value via stationary Shapley pricing, and budget over-consumption.
The proposed solution is a three-layer architecture designed to unify the treatment of these temporal and coordination challenges.
Key technical components include applying neural-ODE temporal decay to shortcut edges and maintaining explicit public/private data separation.

stories.title: Training-Free Looped Transformers
stories.author: Qiang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces 'training-free looped transformers' to enhance model performance.
The method retrofits recurrence onto frozen, pretrained models at inference time without fine-tuning.
Highlights that naive block reapplication degrades performance, emphasizing the need for careful loop implementation.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling to build reliable AI capable of counterfactual reasoning.
It introduces FM-CGM, a modular framework designed to integrate causal reasoning with pretrained foundation models.
The framework aims to leverage the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of large models for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a stronger teacher model is required for effective student training.
Investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong) in LLM pretraining.
Suggests that proper mixing of language modeling techniques can allow for successful distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new methodology for developing AI agent skills, moving beyond current hand-crafted or loosely controlled self-revision methods.
The core idea is to train the agent's skill as an external state, applying optimization discipline similar to weight-space optimization in deep learning.
The paper claims to be the first systematic, controllable text-space optimization approach for agent skills.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: ETCHR addresses the bottleneck in multimodal LLMs where purely textual Chain-of-Thought struggles with fine-grained visual reasoning.
The research proposes a novel approach by decoupling reasoning from image generation, utilizing a dedicated image editing model.
This method aims to overcome limitations in existing 'think with images' paradigms, such as fixed toolkits or noisy intermediate images.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a 'strong' teacher model is necessary for effective student training.
Investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong).
Suggests that proper mixing during language modeling can allow for successful distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: Decomposing Queries into Tool Calls for Long-Video Keyframe Retrieval
stories.author: Derek Hoiem
bullet_points: Introduces ToolMerge, a new method for long-video keyframe retrieval to support video QA.
The method uses an LLM-based planner to decompose complex user queries into specific tool calls.
It addresses limitations of existing methods by merging the results of decomposed queries for more accurate frame selection.

stories.title: ChartFI: Benchmarking Faithfulness and Insightfulness of Chart Descriptions from Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Siming Chen
bullet_points: The paper introduces ChartFI to benchmark the faithfulness and insightfulness of chart descriptions generated by Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Current benchmarks are criticized for using simple, homogeneous charts and shallow, fact-enumeration-based descriptions.
Accurate chart descriptions are crucial for accessibility, cross-modal retrieval, and data insight extraction.

stories.title: Show HN: Geomatic – a command-driven geometry studio enabled with autodiff
stories.author: nivter
bullet_points: Geomatic is a new command-driven geometry studio that integrates autodifferentiation.
It allows users to define geometric primitives (like lines) using simple command syntax, similar to mathematical expressions.
The tool supports advanced features like NumPy/PyTorch-style broadcasting and backpropagation for geometric computations.

stories.title: How to Evaluate AI Agents: LLM-as-Judge Tutorial
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: Introduces LLM-as-Judge as a method for evaluating AI agent quality.
Focuses on detecting critical issues like silent failures, wasted tokens, and hallucinations.
Provides a practical Python tutorial for implementing trajectory analysis in agent evaluation.

stories.title: Multi-Repo Microservice Changes Are a Coordination Problem. I Solved It With AI Agent Teams.
stories.author: ramcsamal
bullet_points: Addresses the complex coordination challenges inherent in multi-repo microservice architectures.
Introduces RepoOrch, an open-source Claude Code plugin designed to manage these multi-repo workspaces.
Leverages AI Agent Teams to automate and solve the coordination problems in distributed development environments.
